title: cistus heterophyllus var. asperifolius
intro: Cistus heterophyllus var. asperifolius, also known as rockrose, is a shrub-like plant in the family Cistaceae, characterized by its thorny stems and fragrant, white to pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and terpenoids. Rockrose has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. Rockrose is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential effects and interactions.
